title: Browser Product Quality Receipts
summary: A validator and receipt contract for proving generated products work in the browser with screenshots, traces, accessibility, mobile, performance, and build evidence.

retrieval.description: Use for generated UI/runtime quality gates that must prove real browser behavior and not just compile success.

donorSources.label: Playwright
donorSources.notes: Primary browser validator runner and trace/screenshot engine.

donorSources.label: web-vitals
donorSources.notes: Real-user LCP, INP, and CLS receipts.

prompt: A frontend is not complete until a real browser receipt proves it loads, responds, and preserves layout on mobile.
Generated apps must store validator evidence: screenshots, traces, console/network logs, a11y output, and build/test logs.
Use Playwright-style flows for core journeys and CDP-style evidence for deterministic screenshots or low-level protocol checks.
Accessibility and reduced-motion checks are product quality gates, not polish.
requiredElements: Browser smoke flow for the primary journey
Accessibility scan or documented manual fallback
forbiddenPatterns: claiming frontend completion without browser evidence
a11y treated as optional for public or customer UI
allowedSubstitutions: Use Chromium CDP directly when Playwright is not available in the environment.
Use manual browser screenshots only as a temporary fallback and mark the receipt as manual.
Use Lighthouse CI in ship-ready lanes; skip it for fast prototype lanes with an explicit note.
stackHints: Store receipts under .retrobuilder/browser-artifacts or future pack-receipts.
Use role/text/test-id locators rather than brittle CSS selectors.
Record traces on failure or retry.

validators.description: The generated frontend must load and complete its primary action without runtime errors.
validators.evidence: Playwright/CDP flow log, screenshot, console log, and network log.

validators.description: Mobile viewports must not clip controls, overflow horizontally, or lose action order.
validators.evidence: Screenshots at 375, 390, or 430 px width plus trace notes.

validators.description: Automated accessibility issues should be detected and either fixed or explicitly waived.
validators.evidence: axe JSON, affected nodes, and before/after screenshots.

validators.description: Ship-ready lanes should include lab or field performance evidence.
validators.evidence: Lighthouse CI report or web-vitals summary.

provenance.notes: Seeded from Product DNA validator research.
